codeReview.summary: The vscode-extension skeleton is incomplete. package.json is missing all required fields (activationEvents, contributes, dependencies). README.md does not exist. npm install passes but only because package.json has no dependencies.

issues.description: Missing 'activationEvents' field. A VS Code extension requires activationEvents to define when the extension activates (e.g., onLanguage, onCommand, onStartupFinished).
Missing 'contributes' field. A VS Code extension requires contributes to define language configurations, diagnostics, commands, or other extension contributions.
Missing 'dependencies' or 'devDependencies' field. A VS Code extension needs at minimum @types/vscode as a devDependency for TypeScript type checking.
README.md does not exist. The validation contract requires README.md explaining: (1) what the extension does, (2) how to install from source, (3) how to use each feature.

summary: Feature write-vscode-extension-skeleton FAILS review. The package.json has only name, version, description, and main fields but is missing all VS Code extension-specific required fields (activationEvents, contributes, dependencies). README.md is entirely absent. While npm install passes (trivially, since there are no dependencies), the skeleton does not constitute a valid VS Code extension. The feature fulfills none of its validation assertions (VAL-TOOL-005, VAL-TOOL-006).
